mnemonics: H0: Null Hypothesis (No Difference), H1: Alternative Hypothesis (Has a Difference)
P-Hacker: P-hacking is manipulating data to get a significant p-value.
Type I Error: False Positive (Rejecting a true null). Type II Error: False Negative (Failing to reject a false null).
keyPoints: The null hypothesis (H0) is a statement of 'no effect' or 'no difference' that you try to find evidence against.
The p-value is the probability of obtaining results at least as extreme as the observed results, assuming the null hypothesis is true. A small p-value (typically ≤ 0.05) is evidence against H0.
Statistical significance (rejecting H0) does not imply practical importance or the size of an effect.
commonMistakes: Confusing statistical significance with practical significance or effect size.
Failing to check assumptions of the chosen test (e.g., normality for a t-test).
Incorrectly stating the conclusion: 'Accepting the null hypothesis' instead of 'failing to reject the null hypothesis'.
